dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 for a report of an unknown problem and a person down at --Address Redacted-- in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Medic Taylor from Rescue Unit 4. What can you tell me about the patient?
Found a 18-year-old male awake but mute and confused in his truck. Chief complaint is altered mental status.
Patient is awake but not responding to verbal commands and is confused.
Airway is open and clear. No signs of obstruction or distress.
Respiratory rate is 23, even and not labored. No cyanosis or use of accessory muscles.
Blood pressure is 144 over 81, and pulse is 122-tachycardia noted.
O2 saturation is 100% on room air.
Blood glucose was 39 mg/dL as per the fire department's report upon initial response.
Glucose improved to 112 mg/dL after administration of 150 mL of D10W IV.
Medication history includes Humalog insulin. Allergy list is negative for drugs.
Found in his pick-up truck at the Belvedere and Cary intersection. No signs of trauma or incident reported by FD.
Skin is diaphoretic but no hives, bruises, or rash present on initial visual inspection.
No head or neck trauma noted to this point. Fire co reported altered mental status and no injuries detected.
ECG findings show sinus tachycardia at the initial stage.
EKG at first check showed sinus tach, no other arrhythmias reported on field strip.
IV established via extremity vein with D10W, 150 mL administered initially.
Glucose administered intravenously to address hypoglycemia at 39 mg/dL.
Respiratory rate decreased slightly to 22, still unlabored and regular.
Heart rate remains at 121, tachycardia still persists.
Blood pressure elevated further to 155 over 89.
Transport is being prioritized for Chippenham Hospital emergency department.
Patient secured onto the cot and then into the ambulance. Note: slid from cot to bed during transit.
Glucose levels at 112 mg/dL now-hypoglycemia has resolved, hyperglycemia is a new concern.
Recheck vitals: 121 pulse, 22 breathe, BP 155/89, and 100% O2.
Hyperglycemia now present, but no acute signs of DKA reported yet.
Patient has insulin-dependent diabetes and is currently using Humalog.
No pain reported. The patient was not able to communicate but did not show signs of agitation or discomfort.
Pain assessment score of zero as documented in PCR notes.
Full report on patient status, interventions, and changes transferred to the ED nurse at arrival.
